situational_context: Active rebuild, Week 1 of return-to-running protocol (first run May 3 after 182-day gap). Chronic load climbing from ultra-low base (17.8→23.46, 1.46% of 2023 peak). ACWR normalized at 1.055 after May 1 gravel spike. HRV stable (38 weekly avg), RHR improved significantly (53→49 bpm). Training readiness high (92). Weight trending down at safe rate (-2.1 kg over 18 days, 104.78 kg). Life-constrained by young baby (4 months) and 3x/week sur site ebike commuting, but training engagement clearly increasing with gravel ride and first comeback run logged. Nutrition: protein consistently below 150g target. No injuries or pain reported post-run.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Retour running #2 OU Endurance vélo Z2

recommendations.text: Continue return-to-running protocol: execute Saturday's run #2 (20-25 min, Z1-Z2, soft surface, Bois de Vincennes) if zero pain from May 3 run. If any localized pain, swap to 45 min bike Z2.
recommendations.measurable: Complete 2nd return-to-running session by May 10 with 0 pain episodes lasting >24h post-run

recommendations.text: Increase daily protein to ≥140g. Current 7d avg is ~119g vs 150g target. Add high-protein breakfast (30g+) and post-training snack (20g+).
recommendations.measurable: Average daily protein ≥140g over next 7 days

recommendations.text: Maintain weight trajectory at -0.5 to -0.7 kg/week. Current rate is safe for tendon adaptation during running rebuild. Do not accelerate deficit beyond -1 kg/week.
recommendations.measurable: Weight ≤104.5 kg at next Withings scan (May 10), no single-week drop >1 kg

markers_to_watch.threshold: Zero localized pain lasting >24h after Saturday's run #2
markers_to_watch.reason: Return-to-running at 104.78 kg after 182-day gap. Tissue tolerance is the bottleneck per protocol.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 30+ over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: At 23.46 (1.46% of 2023 peak). Ebike commutes + structured sessions building base.
RHR improved from 53 to 49 bpm, reflecting improving fitness. Reversal would indicate overtraining or illness.
